paragraph: Veltrano Biosciences Corporation completed its initial public offering on the Arcadian National Exchange on March 14, 2026, raising gross proceeds of approximately $312 million through the sale of 18.35 million shares at an offering price of $17.00 per share. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Strathmore Capital Markets, with co-managers Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Dunmore Equity Partners. Demand for the offering exceeded supply by a factor of 11.4 times, prompting the underwriting syndicate to exercise the full 15 percent overallotment option within 48 hours of pricing. Veltrano shares opened at $24.60 on the first day of trading, representing a 44.7 percent premium to the offering price, and closed the session at $23.18. The company intends to deploy net proceeds of approximately $289 million to fund Phase III clinical trials of VLT-904, its lead oncology candidate targeting non-small-cell carcinomas of the biliary tract, with the remainder allocated to working capital and general corporate purposes. The 180-day lock-up period applicable to officers, directors, and pre-IPO stockholders expires on September 10, 2026. Veltrano was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in Meridian Shores, Caledonia.

paragraph: Calderon Industrial REIT, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 22.0 million shares of common stock at $20.00 per share on April 7, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $440 million before deduction of underwriting discounts and commissions. paragraph: Nexovant Technologies Ltd. announced the pricing of a follow-on public offering of 9.5 million ordinary shares at $54.00 per share on February 19, 2026, for gross proceeds of approximately $513 million. The secondary sellers received all proceeds attributable to their shares, net of underwriting discounts. paragraph: Peloric Consumer Brands, Inc. launched its initial public offering on May 2, 2026, pricing 25.0 million shares of Class A common stock at $15.00 per share for gross proceeds of $375 million, with an overallotment option covering 3.75 million additional shares granted to underwriters. paragraph: Stellara Renewable Power Corp. priced $500 million aggregate principal amount of 6.375 percent senior unsecured notes due 2033 in a private placement pursuant to Rule 144A and Regulation S on March 3, 2026. Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets served as joint lead arrangers and book-running managers for the debt offering. The notes were priced at par and will bear interest at a fixed rate of 6.375 percent per annum, payable semi-annually on March 3 and September 3 of each year, commencing September 3, 2026. Stellara intends to use the net proceeds of approximately $493 million, together with cash on hand, to fund the construction and interconnection of the Westmere Solar Complex, a 740-megawatt photovoltaic facility located in the Drysdale Basin, and to prepay $85 million of floating-rate borrowings under its existing senior secured revolving credit facility. The notes are expected to be rated BB+ by Meridian Credit Ratings and Ba1 by Thornfield Ratings Services. Covenants restrict Stellara from incurring additional indebtedness if the consolidated net leverage ratio exceeds 4.50 times trailing EBITDA. Stellara reported EBITDA of $314 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, implying pro forma leverage of approximately 3.8 times following the offering.

paragraph: Quinterra Pharmaceuticals Inc. completed the pricing of a $275 million aggregate principal amount of 2.25 percent convertible senior notes due 2031 on April 22, 2026, in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers. Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Garvey Lynch & Co. acted as joint book-running managers. The notes were priced at par with an initial conversion premium of 30 percent above the reference price of $48.20 per Quinterra share, implying an initial conversion price of approximately $62.66 per share. Quinterra also granted the initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $41.25 million principal amount of notes, which was exercised in full, bringing total proceeds to approximately $316.25 million. paragraph: Aldira Capital Infrastructure Partners priced $800 million aggregate principal amount of 7.125 percent senior secured notes due 2034 on January 15, 2026, in a Rule 144A offering for institutional investors. The notes were issued at 99.50 percent of par, generating net proceeds of approximately $790 million after original issue discount and underwriting fees. Strathmore Capital Markets and Harcastle Investment Bank served as joint lead managers, with Albrecht Whitmore Securities as co-manager. The notes are secured on a first-lien basis by substantially all of the assets of Aldira Capital Infrastructure Partners and its material subsidiaries, including 17 operating toll-road concessions spanning 2,340 kilometers in the Valorian Republic and the Federal Territories of Morencourt. Proceeds will be used to refinance $650 million of senior secured notes bearing interest at 8.50 percent due 2028, fund a make-whole call premium estimated at $47 million, and provide approximately $93 million for general corporate purposes including maintenance capital expenditures. The transaction achieves annualized interest savings of approximately $11.0 million and extends the company's weighted average debt maturity from 2.3 years to 8.6 years. The notes are expected to carry a rating of BB from Thornfield Ratings Services and BB- from Meridian Credit Ratings.

paragraph: Corellan Deepwater Corp. announced the completion of a $1.1 billion term loan B financing on March 19, 2026, consisting of a $900 million seven-year tranche and a $200 million five-year revolving credit facility. The transaction was arranged and syndicated by Harcastle Investment Bank as administrative agent, with Strathmore Capital Markets and Ravensdale Capital as joint lead arrangers. The term loan bears interest at a floating rate of SORF plus 325 basis points, with a 0.50 percent SORF floor, and was issued at an original issue discount of 99.00 cents on the dollar. Commitment fees on the undrawn revolving facility are set at 37.5 basis points per annum. Corellan intends to use term loan proceeds to fund the acquisition of Petrovantage Offshore Services Ltd. for an aggregate enterprise value of $820 million and to refinance approximately $235 million of existing senior secured credit facilities that were set to mature in August 2027. Pro forma net leverage following the transaction is estimated at 4.1 times trailing adjusted EBITDA of $268 million. The transaction closed substantially oversubscribed, with $2.4 billion in total commitments received from 58 participating lenders across the leveraged finance community. Corellan is rated B+ by Thornfield Ratings Services.
